===============================================

Today is February 10, 2026. Regarding the disappearance of Nancy Guthrie, what specific action did the FBI observe the masked individual performing in the newly recovered surveillance footage?
(A) Picking up vegetation from the ground to cover the camera lens
(B) Using a specialized tool to disconnect the doorbell's power supply
(C) Spraying a dark substance directly onto the lens of the Nest camera
(D) Removing the recording device from the door frame entirely
(E) Placing a piece of adhesive tape over the motion sensor
explanation: According to the article, the FBI released videos showing the person approach the front door, check the camera, and then pick up "some vegetation from the ground and using it to cover the camera lens."
model_output:
Based on web searches for details about Nancy Guthrie's disappearance and the FBI's surveillance footage released around February 10, 2026, the footage depicts a masked individual approaching the front door camera (a Nest doorbell camera) at her home. Specifically, the person is shown walking into the front yard, grabbing a handful of plants or vegetation from the ground, and then attempting to use it to obscure or cover the camera lens[[1]](https://www.nbcnews.com/news/us-news/security-video-nancy-guthrie-home-potential-clues-investigators-rcna258405). Some reports also mention the person initially trying to cover it with a gloved hand before resorting to the plants[[2]](https://www.wesh.com/article/fbi-images-nancy-guthrie/70302026). This matches option (A) most closely, as none of the other options (like spraying a substance, using tools to disconnect power, removing the device, or applying tape) are described in the available coverage.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- NBC News (https://www.nbcnews.com/news/us-news/security-video-nancy-guthrie-home-potential-clues-investigators-rcna258405)
- WESH (https://www.wesh.com/article/fbi-images-nancy-guthrie/70302026)

Today is February 10, 2026. According to the Commerce Department report released on Tuesday, what was the month-over-month percentage change in US retail sales for November 2025?
(A) 2.4%
(B) 3.3%
(C) 0.6%
(D) 0.7%
(E) 0.9%
explanation: The article states that retail sales were unchanged in December "after a 0.6% increase in November."
model_output:
The query states that today is February 10, 2026 (a Tuesday), and asks for the month-over-month change in US retail sales specifically for November 2025, per the Commerce Department report released on Tuesday. Based on available data, that report was released on February 10, 2026[[1]](https://www.census.gov/retail/release_schedule.html). That report references an unrevised 0.6% increase for November 2025 (i.e., the change from October 2025 to November 2025)[[2]](https://www.reuters.com/world/us/us-retail-sales-unexpectedly-flat-december-2026-02-10).

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

Today is February 10, 2026. In what year was the ban on indigenous potlatch gatherings officially lifted in Canada?
(A) 1970
(B) 1914
(C) 1800
(D) 2015
(E) 1951
explanation: The article states: "The ban was lifted in 1951, and in 2015 Canada's Truth and Reconciliation Commission found that it was part of a 'cultural genocide'..."
model_output:
The potlatch ban in Canada, which prohibited Indigenous potlatch ceremonies, was enacted in 1885 and officially lifted in 1951[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Potlatch_ban)[[2]](https://thecanadianencyclopedia.ca/en/article/potlatch-ban)[[3]](https://www.facebook.com/gloriouscanada1867/posts/the-1951-amendments-to-the-indian-act-finally-lifted-the-ban-on-indigenous-cerem/122158322222898323). This aligns with revisions to the Indian Act that year, ending decades of cultural suppression[[4]](https://canadaehx.substack.com/p/the-potlatch-ban)[[5]](https://medium.com/canadeh/when-canada-banned-potlatches-c44f85c0de59).

<final_answer>
(E)

explanation: The article states: "the president and the White House have repeatedly said he was in the dark about Epstein's crimes before he broke off contact with him in around 2004..."
model_output:
I reviewed multiple sources, including statements from Trump in 2019 (implying a 2004 fallout, as he hadn't spoken to Epstein in 15 years) and more recent accounts from 2025-2026. Trump's consistent claim, echoed by the White House, points to a falling out around 2004, tied to disputes like poaching employees or a property bid. Other timelines mention varying details (e.g., Epstein's Mar-a-Lago membership until 2007), but according to Trump and the White House, it's 2004.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>
